description_long: This is a pair of tall, rectangular office buildings with a modern, utilitarian design, featuring a repetitive grid of windows and concrete or metal paneling. The buildings are closely spaced, with one of them exhibiting a large, irregular section of structural damage near the upper floors, exposing rough concrete and internal framework. The facades are dominated by a regular pattern of square windows, separated by horizontal and vertical bands, giving a modular appearance. The rooftops are flat and unadorned, except for a single antenna or spire on the taller building. The overall color palette is muted, with shades of gray and blue, and there is no visible signage or decorative elements. The buildings appear to be constructed primarily from reinforced concrete and glass, typical of mid-to-late 20th-century commercial architecture.
description: A pair of modern, rectangular office buildings with grid-like window patterns and a large damaged section on one tower, finished in gray and blue tones.
description_view_0: Two office buildings side by side, with the right building showing a large damaged area on its upper right corner.
description_view_1: The damaged section is now on the left building, clearly exposing internal structure, while the right building remains intact.
description_view_2: Rear view showing the flat rooftops and the antenna on the taller building, with no visible damage from this angle.
description_view_3: Side view highlighting the depth of the buildings and the damaged area on the left building's upper edge.
attribute_window_pattern: Highly regular, grid-like arrangement of square windows in horizontal and vertical bands, evenly spaced across all facades.
